title: Algorithm and interpretation
text: transport plan PP minimises: ∑ijPijDij+ε⋅KL(P∥𝐚⊗𝐛)+ρq⋅KL(P𝟏∥𝐚)+ρr⋅KL(P⊤𝟏∥𝐛)\sum_{ij} P_{ij} D_{ij}   + \varepsilon \cdot \mathrm{KL}(P \,\|\, \mathbf{}\otimes\mathbf{b})   + \rho_q \cdot \mathrm{KL}(P\mathbf{1} \,\|\, \mathbf{})   + \rho_r \cdot \mathrm{KL}(P^\top\!\mathbf{1} \,\|\, \mathbf{b}) 𝐚\mathbf{} 𝐛\mathbf{b} query reference node masses. KL marginal penalties relax balance constraint: mass can destroyed rather transported, query node good reference counterpart routes small fraction mass rather forcing artificial match. plan found internal pure-R Sinkhorn scaling implementation.

title: Algorithm and interpretation
text: match_fraction near 1 means node transported mass reference nodes; near 0 means little overlap reference population. Label transfer rejected low-match nodes.

title: Algorithm and interpretation
text: query node ii OT plan defines weighted mean displacement toward matched reference nodes: Δi=∑jPij(Cref,j−Cquery,)∑jPij\Delta_i = \frac{\displaystyle\sum_j P_{ij}\,(C_{\text{ref},j} - C_{\text{query},})}                   {\displaystyle\sum_j P_{ij}}  small ∥Δi∥\|\Delta_i\| means query node sits close matched reference positions. large value indicates systematic offset — potentially batch effect true population shift — worth examining accepting corrected projection.

title: Algorithm and interpretation
text: Every sample query node ii shifted Δi\Delta_i re-projected: corrected_som_units=arg⁡mink∥(xs+Δi)−Cref,k∥\text{corrected\_som\_unit}_s = \arg\min_k \|(x_s + \Delta_i) - C_{\text{ref},k}\|  assignments reported results table. old_som_unit (blue) primary result; corrected_som_unit (red), corrected_som_distance, corrected_outside_reference_distance intended visualisation triage, replacement assignments.

title: Algorithm and interpretation
text: query node ii, reference node highest transported mass row P[,⋅]P[, \cdot] identified dominant match, label transferred acceptance thresholds passed.  Transfer rejected two gates: match_fraction < 0.05 (node meaningful reference overlap) transferred_label_confidence < 0.60 (dominant reference node mixed assign clean label).

title: Algorithm and interpretation
text: OT-derived columns auxiliary. Three structural limitations govern trusted.

title: Algorithm and interpretation
text: correction target node ii transport-weighted mean matched reference codebook vectors: Ĉ=∑jPijCref,j∑jPij\hat{C}_i = \frac{\sum_j P_{ij}\,C_{\text{ref},j}}{\sum_j P_{ij}} conditional mean, corrected positions always lie inside convex hull reference codebook. Two consequences follow. First, variance shrinks: query nodes genuinely sit near periphery reference cluster pulled inward. Second, dense reference regions attract mass — reference one large T-cell cluster one small NK cluster, even predominantly NK query node correction vector biased toward T-cell centroid. Contraction grows epsilon increases, larger entropic penalty spreads transport row P[,⋅]P[, \cdot] across reference nodes. Interpret corrected_som_unit correction_norm: large shift (∥Δi∥\|\Delta_i\|) high-epsilon fit suspect combination.

Anchor-based methods CytoNorm exploit exactly pairing, using anchor controls measured old new batches identify batch map minimal ambiguity. somalign designed common case anchors exist: phenotypic structure populations (encoded codebooks) shared. remeasured anchor controls, incorporate reference-building stage rather relying OT correction.

title: Algorithm and interpretation
text: match_fraction (derived label-transfer gate) reflects ε\varepsilon/ρ\rho regime much biological novelty. low match_fraction means little query mass reached reference nodes, can happen () query contains genuinely novel population, (b) rho_query small, (c) epsilon large, (d) query SOM topology differs reference. null model calibrating much destroyed mass constitutes evidence novelty. Use somalign_sensitivity_grid() assess whether low-match nodes stable across ε\varepsilon/ρ\rho values interpreting novel populations.

title: Algorithm and interpretation
text: Direct projection columns primary result. OT-derived columns auxiliary — always read alongside direct assignment somalign_diagnostics() output. OT runs M×KM \times K codebook matrix. per-sample cost O(n⋅K)O(n \cdot K) nearest-node search, performed configurable chunks somalign_fit() keep memory use predictable.

title: Anchor-regularized alignment with remeasured samples
text: new batch carries modest uniform batch offset – realistic reagent lot changes inter-instrument calibration drift.
code: library(kohonen) library(somalign)  set.seed(42) p <- 5L  # Old batch: two well-separated populations old_data <- rbind(   matrix(rnorm(60 * p, mean = -2, sd = 0.6), ncol = p),   matrix(rnorm(60 * p, mean =  2, sd = 0.6), ncol = p) ) colnames(old_data) <- paste0("CD", seq_len(p))  # Train reference SOM and build reference object in one call reference <- somalign_train_reference(   old_data,   grid = kohonen::somgrid(4, 4, "hexagonal"),   rlen = 50 ) reference #> <somalign_reference> #>   features: 5 #>   reference nodes: 16 #>   labelled nodes: 0 shift    <- rep(0.2, p) new_data <- old_data + matrix(shift, nrow = nrow(old_data), ncol = p, byrow = TRUE)  query <- somalign_query(   new_data, reference,   grid = kohonen::somgrid(4, 4, "hexagonal"),   rlen = 50 ) #> somalign_reference_from_som: SOM has no second code layer; label transfer will be disabled. query #> <somalign_query> #>   samples: 120 #>   features: 5 #>   query nodes: 16

title: Anchor-regularized alignment with remeasured samples
text: rho_anchor controls much anchor bonus reduces normalized OT cost. rho_anchor = 0, bonus vanishes result identical somalign_fit(). rho_anchor grows, anchor-supported pairs become progressively cheaper. bonus exceeds normalized cost pair, pair’s effective cost clamped zero – plan spreads mass among zero-cost pairs according entropic regularization rather differential anchor support, large values continue concentrate mass. practical upper bound rho_anchor * max_A / n_anchors <= 1, max_A highest anchor count single node pair. Values range 0.5–2 reasonable starting point. coverage sparse (< 30% nodes) anchor pairs unevenly distributed, smaller value avoids -weighting node pairs. targeted diagnostic, compare somalign_diagnostics() node-level match_fraction correction_norm across values, use somalign_results() check whether corrected node assignments shift direction consistent biological expectations.

See diagnostics$ot$match_mass_ratio for details. do.call(rbind, sweep_results) #>   rho_anchor transport_mass mean_corr_norm #> 1        0.5        1.08310        0.29885 #> 2        1.0        1.09137        0.29530 #> 3        1.5        1.09759        0.29356 #> 4        2.0        1.10413        0.29233 #> 5        4.0        1.12458        0.29385

title: Anchor-regularized alignment with remeasured samples
text: default correction = "cost_bonus" applies OT-derived shift across features. reagent- calibration-driven batch effects correct — displacement genuinely technical removed. assumption breaks new batch contains populations activation states absent reference: full-space shift pushes cells toward reference coordinates erases distinguishing biology. anchor pair links biological unit measured batches, row vector anchor_old - anchor_new direct observation batch displacement biological contribution. SVD n_anchors × p displacement matrix isolates true batch directions — call V_batch. Restricting node shifts V_batch corrects batch component; biology orthogonal V_batch preserved. batch_subspace element carries V (p × rank), rank, variance_explained. default variance_threshold = 0.9 selects smallest rank r top r squared singular values account least 90% total. pure uniform offset collapses rank 1; heterogeneous multi-directional batch effect occupies dimensions. Changing variance_threshold adjusts rank without altering anchor displacement data. correction = "" applies anchor cost bonus OT solve restricts resulting shifts V_batch: rho_anchor effect correction = "subspace" (cost bonus apply). tuning guidelines "cost_bonus" apply "". simple, uniform batch offset three modes produce similar corrected positions; difference becomes meaningful anchor coverage uneven query carries populations represented reference.
code:
fit_sub <- somalign_fit_anchored(   query, reference,   anchor_old = anchor_old,   anchor_new = anchor_new,   correction = "subspace" ) #> somalign_fit: 13 query node(s) have match_mass_ratio > 1 (max 1.29); this is expected in unbalanced OT. See diagnostics$ot$match_mass_ratio for details. cat("Correction mode:  ", fit_sub$anchors$correction, "
") #> Correction mode:   subspace cat("Batch subspace V: ", nrow(fit_sub$anchors$batch_subspace$V), "rows x",                           ncol(fit_sub$anchors$batch_subspace$V), "cols

") #> Rank:              1 cat("Variance explained:", round(fit_sub$anchors$batch_subspace$variance_explained, 3), "
") #> Variance explained: 1 fit_sub_full <- somalign_fit_anchored(   query, reference,   anchor_old        = anchor_old,   anchor_new        = anchor_new,   correction        = "subspace",   variance_threshold = 1.0 ) #> somalign_fit: 13 query node(s) have match_mass_ratio > 1 (max 1.29); this is expected in unbalanced OT. See diagnostics$ot$match_mass_ratio for details. cat("Rank at threshold = 1.0:", fit_sub_full$anchors$batch_subspace$rank, "

See diagnostics$ot$match_mass_ratio for details. cat("Correction mode:", fit_both$anchors$correction, "

title: Anchor-regularized alignment with remeasured samples
text: somalign_fit_anchored() appropriate set samples — QC pools, reference standards, proficiency panels — physically run old new batches, providing ground-truth node-pair correspondences. Anchor pairs need cover full codebook; even sparse coverage meaningfully constrains OT plan covered node pairs, uncovered nodes fall back standard objective. remeasured samples available, use somalign_fit() directly. Fabricated imputed anchor pairs add information misguide transport. query-biology must preserved, use correction = "subspace" "". full-space shift appropriate — batch dominates signal-preservation needed — default "cost_bonus" sufficient. See vignette("two-pass", package = "somalign") two-pass decomposition approach, handles large global offsets without requiring remeasured anchors.

Labels transferred nodes treated provisional. diagnostics$ot$match_mass_ratio stores unclipped transported-mass ratio; values 1 can occur unbalanced OT errors. diagnostics$ot$transport_mass tells much total query mass OT plan moved reference nodes (opposed absorbing via KL margin penalties). diagnostics$ot$max_row_mass_error captures worst per-node marginal deviation; large value means unbalanced fit allowed substantial mass destruction, appropriate truly novel query content visible QC records. diagnostics$projection fractions report many samples outside saved reference distance thresholds OT-derived correction. title: Quick start
text: user-facing workflow built around small set exported functions: Build reference somalign_train_reference(), wrap saved artifacts somalign_reference() / somalign_reference_from_nodes(). Prepare query data somalign_query(). Query samples always scaled reference center scale. Align query reference SOM nodes somalign_fit(). default internal solver used solver = "internal" compatibility alias solver = "auto". Extract per-sample output somalign_results(). Pass somalign_results(fit, data = sample_metadata) append one metadata row per query sample. Inspect fit quality somalign_diagnostics() tune OT parameters somalign_sensitivity_grid().

title: Two-pass global and local alignment
text: new batch carries clear global offset plus smaller within-population noise:
code: library(kohonen) library(somalign)  set.seed(42) p <- 4L  old_data <- rbind(   matrix(rnorm(60 * p, mean = -2, sd = 0.5), ncol = p),   matrix(rnorm(60 * p, mean =  2, sd = 0.5), ncol = p) ) colnames(old_data) <- paste0("CD", seq_len(p)) old_labels <- rep(c("pop_A", "pop_B"), each = 60L)  reference <- somalign_train_reference(   old_data,   labels = old_labels,   grid   = kohonen::somgrid(3, 3, "hexagonal"),   rlen   = 30 ) reference #> <somalign_reference> #>   features: 4 #>   reference nodes: 9 #>   labelled nodes: 7 global_shift <- c(0.4, 0.3, 0.35, 0.25)  set.seed(43) local_noise <- matrix(rnorm(nrow(old_data) * p, 0, 0.1), ncol = p) new_data    <- old_data +   matrix(global_shift, nrow(old_data), p, byrow = TRUE) + local_noise  query <- somalign_query(   new_data, reference,   grid = kohonen::somgrid(3, 3, "hexagonal"),   rlen = 30 ) #> somalign_reference_from_som: SOM has no second code layer; label transfer will be disabled. query #> <somalign_query> #>   samples: 120 #>   features: 4 #>   query nodes: 9

title: Two-pass global and local alignment
text: $two_pass slot records estimated global shift epsilon values: global_shift points correction direction — query codebook toward reference — sign opposite true batch offset. node shifts mass-weighted mean first-pass OT transport vectors, magnitude reference-scaled units, raw units, directly equal raw feature offset. matters practice direction: feature’s sign oppose known batch offset, features larger displacements show larger absolute values. norm global_shift_norm gives overall scalar measure global batch displacement can compared across batches experiments.
code:
fit2 <- somalign_fit_two_pass(   query, reference,   epsilon_global = 0.3,   epsilon_local  = 0.1 ) #> somalign_fit: 6 query node(s) have match_mass_ratio > 1 (max 1.41); this is expected in unbalanced OT. See diagnostics$ot$match_mass_ratio for details. #> somalign_fit: 6 query node(s) have match_mass_ratio > 1 (max 1.08); this is expected in unbalanced OT. See diagnostics$ot$match_mass_ratio for details. fit2 #> <somalign_fit> #>   solver: internal #>   query nodes: 9 #>   reference nodes: 9 #>   transport mass: 1.053 cat("Estimated global shift (correction direction, query -> reference):
") #> Estimated global shift (correction direction, query -> reference): print(round(fit2$two_pass$global_shift, 3)) #>    CD1    CD2    CD3    CD4  #> -0.165 -0.132 -0.153 -0.094 cat("
True batch offset (positive = new batch above old):
") #>  #> True batch offset (positive = new batch above old): print(round(global_shift, 3)) #> [1] 0.40 0.30 0.35 0.25 cat("
Global shift magnitude:", round(fit2$two_pass$global_shift_norm, 3), "
") #>  #> Global shift magnitude: 0.277

title: Two-pass global and local alignment
text: $two_pass$batch_subspace element summarises principal directions first-pass correction field: read-diagnostic. Rank 1 indicates pass-1 node shifts nearly collinear — expected single global offset dominates. Higher rank means first-pass correction heterogeneous directions across codebook, pointing toward spatially varied batch effect. subspace used correction. Total shift = residual pass-2 shift + global shift g. diagnostic may conflate batch effects biology (first-pass OT sees original unmodified codebook, controlled anchors), read descriptively, geometric estimate true batch direction. anchor-based analogue serve geometric estimate, see vignette("anchor-samples", package = "somalign"). variance_threshold argument controls rank selection diagnostic:
code:
bs <- fit2$two_pass$batch_subspace cat("Batch subspace rank (pass 1):        ", bs$rank, "
") #> Batch subspace rank (pass 1):         2 cat("Variance explained by leading directions:", round(bs$variance_explained, 3), "
") #> Variance explained by leading directions: 0.931 fit2b <- somalign_fit_two_pass(query, reference, variance_threshold = 1.0) #> somalign_fit: 6 query node(s) have match_mass_ratio > 1 (max 1.41); this is expected in unbalanced OT. See diagnostics$ot$match_mass_ratio for details. #> somalign_fit: 6 query node(s) have match_mass_ratio > 1 (max 1.08); this is expected in unbalanced OT. See diagnostics$ot$match_mass_ratio for details. cat("Rank at variance_threshold = 1.0:", fit2b$two_pass$batch_subspace$rank, "
") #> Rank at variance_threshold = 1.0: 4  # Correction is unchanged regardless of variance_threshold cat("Node shifts identical:", isTRUE(all.equal(fit2$node_shifts, fit2b$node_shifts)), "
") #> Node shifts identical: TRUE

title: Two-pass global and local alignment

code:
fit1 <- somalign_fit(query, reference, epsilon = 0.1) #> somalign_fit: 4 query node(s) have match_mass_ratio > 1 (max 1.10); this is expected in unbalanced OT. See diagnostics$ot$match_mass_ratio for details.  cat("Mean node shift -- two-pass: ",     round(mean(sqrt(rowSums(fit2$node_shifts^2))), 4), "
") #> Mean node shift -- two-pass:  0.2982 cat("Mean node shift -- one-pass: ",     round(mean(sqrt(rowSums(fit1$node_shifts^2))), 4), "
") #> Mean node shift -- one-pass:  0.209 res2 <- somalign_results(fit2) res1 <- somalign_results(fit1) cat("Column names match:", identical(names(res2), names(res1)), "
") #> Column names match: TRUE cat("Rows:", nrow(res2), "
") #> Rows: 120  diag2 <- somalign_diagnostics(fit2) cat("Solver converged:", diag2$solver$converged, "

") #> Transport mass:   1.0527

title: Two-pass global and local alignment
text: somalign_fit() somalign_fit_two_pass() accept label_guided = TRUE, applies large cost penalty node pairs discordant dominant labels. reference labels known batch scramble population boundaries, concentrates OT transport onto -label pairs reduces spurious cross-cluster mass. label_guided = TRUE requires query$label_prob non-NULL. standard somalign_query() workflow using kohonen::som() leaves field NULL; populated kohonen::supersom() kohonen::xyf() SOM label code layer passed som_query. Build query SOM one-hot label layer, supply via som_query, set label_guided = TRUE: works equally somalign_fit(). labels noisy batch shifts populations across label boundaries, cost penalty can destabilise OT plan; check somalign_diagnostics() compare somalign_sensitivity_grid() using label-transferred assignments downstream analyses.
code: # One-hot label matrix for new_data (requires knowing new-batch labels) Y <- model.matrix(~ old_labels - 1) colnames(Y) <- levels(factor(old_labels))  # Train a supersom with the label layer new_scaled <- scale(new_data, center = reference$center, scale = reference$scale) query_ss   <- kohonen::supersom(   list(new_scaled, Y),   grid = kohonen::somgrid(3, 3, "hexagonal"),   rlen = 30,   keep.data = TRUE )  query_labeled <- somalign_query(   new_data, reference,   som_query      = query_ss,   codebook_space = "reference_scaled" )  fit_lg <- somalign_fit_two_pass(   query_labeled, reference,   label_guided = TRUE )

title: Two-pass global and local alignment
text: Two-pass alignment useful global batch offset large relative epsilon_local — large enough single pass low epsilon struggles route mass across full displacement, single pass high epsilon loses population-level resolution. explicit global subtraction removes tension. batch shift small OT problem well-conditioned single epsilon, somalign_fit() simpler equally effective. remeasured control samples available, somalign_fit_anchored() correction = "subspace" offers geometrically grounded signal-preserving correction alternative two-pass decomposition.

title: Check query-reference codebook alignment before cell-level computation — somalign_check_codebook_alignment
text: query_codebook Numeric matrix query SOM codebook vectors reference-scaled coordinate space (nodes \(\times\) features).  Column names must include features reference$features. reference somalign_reference object. query_masses Optional numeric vector query node masses (length nrow(query_codebook)).  Used mass-weighted centroid check. NULL, uniform weights assumed. epsilon OT regularisation parameter passed somalign_fit().  Used contextualise cost matrix coverage check.  Default 0.1. stop_if_critical TRUE (default), throw error feature zero range overlap.  Set FALSE emit warning instead return diagnostics.

title: Check query-reference codebook alignment before cell-level computation — somalign_check_codebook_alignment
text: somalign_codebook_check list (returned invisibly) : per_feature Data frame: one row per feature ref_min, ref_max, query_min, query_max, overlap_fraction, centroid_drift, centroid_drift_sd, flag ("ok" / "warning" / "critical"). cost_summary Named numeric vector: median_cost (raw median pairwise squared distance), p95_cost, cost_scale (normalisation factor used somalign_fit), fraction_near_eps (fraction pairs within \(3\varepsilon\) normalised cost). n_critical_features Number features zero overlap. n_warning_features Number features flagged warning. verdict "pass", "warning", "critical".

title: Align a query SOM to a reference SOM — somalign_fit
text: query somalign_query object. reference somalign_reference object. epsilon Entropic regularisation strength. cost matrix normalised median positive entry computing Sinkhorn kernel, epsilon approximately scale- dimension-invariant. default 0.1 gives sharp transport plan preserves cell-type specificity typical z-scored SOM codebooks. Larger values (0.3–0.5) produce smoother, diffuse plans can help convergence noisy high-dimensional data dilute label posteriors increase barycentric shrinkage corrected projection. small values (< 0.05) make transport increasingly discrete may require solver = "log_domain" numerical stability. normalisation scale stored diagnostics$solver$cost_scale. rho_query Query-side unbalanced mass relaxation. rho_ref Reference-side unbalanced mass relaxation. solver Sinkhorn solver variant. "internal" (default) "auto" use primal-domain scaling iteration. "log_domain" uses numerically stable log-potential variant avoids kernel underflow small epsilon high-dimensional codebooks; slower per iteration tolerates cost/epsilon ratios cause "internal" warn. min_match_fraction Minimum transported fraction required query node label transfer accepted. confidence_threshold Minimum top-label probability required query node label transfer accepted. correction_min_mass Minimum transported node mass required correction shift applied. Corrections also require node match fraction pass min_match_fraction. max_iter Maximum internal Sinkhorn iterations. tol Internal Sinkhorn convergence tolerance. chunk_size Integer. Number samples project per chunk computing nearest reference node. Use Inf NULL chunking (allocates full n_samples x n_nodes matrix). Default 10000L. diagonal_boost Non-negative scalar. Amount reduce normalised OT cost query node's nearest reference node. positive value makes transport plan prefer identity-like mappings, shrinking -correction two codebooks already close. Zero (default) leaves cost unchanged. Values around 0.1–0.5 reasonable starting point; large values concentrate mass diagonal plan degrades toward simple nearest-neighbour assignment. label_guided Logical. TRUE, uses query$label_prob reference$label_prob add large cost penalty node pairs whose dominant labels disagree, constraining OT transport mass predominantly concordant cell-type nodes. Nodes maximum label probability 0.5 treated unlabeled never penalized. Errors label_guided = TRUE either label_prob NULL.

text: Correction modes. Three strategies available via correction argument. "cost_bonus" (default, current behaviour): anchor count matrix biases OT cost anchor-supported node pairs cheaper; resulting node shifts applied full feature space. "subspace": batch subspace \(V_{\text{batch}}\) estimated SVD anchor displacement matrix \(D = X_{\text{old}} - X_{\text{new}}\) (n_anchors × p). row \(D\) -biological-unit –measurement, dominant singular vectors isolate true batch direction. Node shifts plain OT solve (cost bonus) projected onto \(V_{\text{batch}}\): batch-direction component applied. Biological variation orthogonal \(V_{\text{batch}}\) preserved. synthetic validation shows orthogonal component survives ~99.7% (1.496 vs ideal 1.500) "cost_bonus" erases . rank \(r\) smallest index cumulative squared singular values reach variance_threshold (default 0.9). \(D\) centred — mean batch direction dominant structure want capture. "": applies cost bonus OT solve restricts resulting shifts \(V_{\text{batch}}\). "subspace" "" expose fit$anchors$batch_subspace (list V, rank, variance_explained). "cost_bonus" sets NULL. Cost modification. Let \(C\) M×K codebook distance matrix normalised median positive entry (somalign_fit()). anchor pairs projected onto query codebook (old batch) reference codebook (new batch), yielding count matrix \(\) \(A_{kl}\) number anchor pairs whose old measurement maps query node \(k\) new measurement maps reference node \(l\). (query SOM trained new-batch data, projecting old-batch anchor onto identifies query node anchor occupied batch shift; projecting new-batch anchor onto reference SOM identifies corresponding reference node shift.) modified cost $$\tilde{C}_{kl} = \max\!\bigl(C_{kl} - \rho_{\mathrm{anchor}} \cdot   A_{kl} / n_{\mathrm{anchors}},\; 0\bigr).$$ Pairs many anchor observations get cost reduced toward zero (free transport), uncovered pairs retain original cost. Non-negativity enforced \(\max(\cdot, 0)\) clamp. Clamp behaviour large rho_anchor. anchor bonus exceeds \(C_{kl}\), effective cost clamped zero. pairs identical effective cost transport mass among determined entropic regularisation alone rather relative anchor counts. clamp required keep costs non-negative; large rho_anchor plan anchor-covered pairs becomes entropic, concentrated. practical upper bound rho_anchor * max() / n_anchors <= 1, .e., even -supported pair reduces cost one median-distance unit. Fallback uncovered nodes. Query nodes anchor samples retain original pairwise costs, transport plan nodes determined entirely OT objective — somalign_fit(). Inspect $anchors$coverage_fraction see fraction query nodes least one anchor pair. Return value. object class c("somalign_anchored_fit", "somalign_fit"), downstream functions accept somalign_fit object (somalign_results(), somalign_diagnostics()) work unchanged. additional $anchors list element attached: n_anchors Number anchor pairs supplied. rho_anchor value rho_anchor used. correction correction mode: "cost_bonus", "subspace", "". nodes_covered Number query nodes ≥ 1 anchor pair. coverage_fraction nodes_covered / nrow(query$codebook). batch_subspace "subspace" "" modes: list V (p × rank matrix), rank (integer), variance_explained (cumulative variance selected rank). NULL "cost_bonus".

title: Global pre-correction of query data to match the reference distribution — somalign_normalize
text: somalign_normalize() applies per-marker shift (optionally rescaling) every cell, population-specific batch effects remain somalign_fit() resolve. use function. Mean-normalisation assumes apparent per-marker shift reflects instrument drift reagent-lot differences affecting populations uniformly. shift reflects genuine compositional differences batches (e.g.\ different cell-type frequencies), subtracting global mean distorts biology.

title: Build a reference object directly from a trained kohonen SOM — somalign_reference_from_som
text: som trained kohonen SOM object (kohonen::som(), kohonen::supersom(), kohonen::xyf()) keep.data = TRUE (kohonen default). center Named numeric vector reference feature centers, one per feature.  Required. scale Named numeric vector reference feature scales, one per feature (must strictly positive).  Required. codebook_space Coordinate system SOM codebook. "reference_scaled" SOM trained data already transformed center scale; "raw" SOM trained raw feature values transformed reference-scaled space use. labels "codebook" (default) reads per-node label distribution Y-layer codes[[2]] enables label transfer.  "none" skips label extraction disables label transfer regardless whether Y-layer present. quantile_probs Quantile levels per-node distance thresholds. Passed somalign_reference_from_nodes. distance_chunk_size Number cells process per chunk computing X-space cell--node distances.  Reduce memory tight; increase faster throughput.  Default 1e6.

title: Build a reference object directly from a trained kohonen SOM — somalign_reference_from_som
text: somalign_reference object, identical structure output somalign_reference built without reprojecting cells.

title: Build a reference object directly from a trained kohonen SOM — somalign_reference_from_som
text: X codebook codes[[1]] — reference node positions feature space, already used existing API. Node masses tabulate(som$unit.classif) — exact counts training cells, zero cost. Label probabilities codes[[2]] — supervised Y-layer codebook xyf/supersom object.  row per-node distribution cell-type labels.  Absent plain som() objects, case label transfer disabled. Distance quantiles Recomputed reference-scaled X-space embedded som$data[[1]] using cell's known unit.classif assignment.  O(N \(\times\) p) argmax O(N \(\times\) nodes) memory peak. Note partition semantics.  xyf/supersom trained equal layer weights, unit.classif reflects joint X+Y (label-weighted) assignment, pure-X nearest-node assignment.  Node masses therefore match SOM's supervised partition rather somalign's X-geometry.  Distance quantiles still computed X-space outside-reference threshold scale somalign_fit()'s query distances.

title: Build a reference object directly from a trained kohonen SOM — somalign_reference_from_som
code: set.seed(1) n <- 120 X <- matrix(rnorm(n * 2), nrow = n, ncol = 2,             dimnames = list(NULL, c("F1", "F2"))) Y <- cbind(A = rep(c(1, 0), each = n / 2),            B = rep(c(0, 1), each = n / 2)) som_obj <- kohonen::supersom(list(X, Y),                              grid = kohonen::somgrid(3, 3, "hexagonal"),                              rlen = 5, keep.data = TRUE) center <- colMeans(X) scale  <- apply(X, 2, sd) X_scaled <- scale(X, center = center, scale = scale) som_scaled <- kohonen::supersom(list(X_scaled, Y),                                 grid = kohonen::somgrid(3, 3, "hexagonal"),                                 rlen = 5, keep.data = TRUE) ref <- somalign_reference_from_som(som_scaled,                                    center = center, scale = scale,                                    codebook_space = "reference_scaled")

title: somalign 0.99.4
text: somalign_query_from_som() — zero-reprojection query constructor reuses som$unit.classif directly per-cell node assignments, bypassing O(N × nodes) nearest-code search somalign_query() performs. Accepts optional pre-transformed codebook (e.g. winsorisation rescaling reference-scaled space) codebook_space argument identical somalign_query(). returned somalign_query object fully compatible somalign_fit(). sample_distance set NA (field read somalign_fit()). codebook non-linearly post-processed, reused assignments approximate: cells near node boundaries may flip, affects small fraction tail cells measurably impact OT alignment quality. somalign_sensitivity_grid() — direct projection cached across grid points. O(N × reference_nodes) projection query cells onto reference codebook identical every (epsilon, rho_query, rho_ref) combination, now computed grid loop reused. K-point grid saves (K − 1) projection passes. function signature otherwise unchanged (somalign_fit() parameters accepted explicitly; ... forwarding removed).

title: somalign 0.99.3
text: somalign_reference_from_som() — zero-reprojection reference constructor trained kohonen SOM objects. Reuses information already stored trained object: node masses som$unit.classif (exact counts every training cell computational cost), label probabilities supervised Y-layer codebook codes[[2]] (enables label transfer without passing labels vector), per-node distance thresholds recomputed reference-scaled X-space embedded som$data[[1]]. distance computation O(N × p) — single subtraction per cell already-known assigned node — argmax O(N × nodes) memory peak. 44.6 M-cell pilot cohort eliminates bottleneck previously required subsampling 100 k cells. existing somalign_reference() somalign_reference_from_nodes() APIs unchanged. Note: xyf/supersom SOM trained equal X+Y layer weights, unit.classif reflects joint supervised assignment. Node masses therefore match SOM’s partition rather pure-X nearest-node assignment. Distance quantiles computed X-space outside-reference thresholds remain scale somalign_fit()’s query distances.

title: somalign 0.99.2
text: Default epsilon lowered 0.5 0.1 somalign_fit(), somalign_fit_anchored(), somalign_som_stability(). cost normalisation added v0.99.1, epsilon = 0.5 left transport plan diffuse: Nuñez 2023 full-spectrum flow CyTOF benchmarks, label posteriors collapsed onto reference class prior CyTOF acceptance dropped zero. epsilon = 0.1 minority classes transfer correctly corrected JS divergence 4× lower. Code sets epsilon explicitly unaffected; relied default, re-check acceptance rates correction norms. Default epsilon_global somalign_fit_two_pass() lowered 0.5 0.3. global pass aligns coarse structure tolerates smoothing local pass, keeps larger epsilon single-pass default.

title: somalign 0.99.2
text: somalign_fit() gains solver = "log_domain" option. log-domain Sinkhorn variant works log-potentials ff (query) gg (reference) rather primal scaling vectors, avoiding exp(-C/epsilon) kernel underflow entirely. slower per iteration tolerates cost/epsilon ratios cause default "internal" solver warn. somalign_som_stability() — new function sweeps vector random seeds query SOM training, holds reference fixed, returns per-seed summary data frame. Reports transport_mass, mean_match_fraction, max_row_mass_error, accepted_label_fraction, outside_direct_fraction, outside_corrected_fraction, mean_correction_norm, converged seed, quantifying run--run variance SOM training randomness — largest uncontrolled variance source somalign workflow.

title: somalign 0.99.2
text: somalign_fit() diagnostics now include two scale-invariant marginal-error fields: diagnostics$solver$rel_marginal_row_error diagnostics$solver$rel_marginal_col_error. normalise absolute marginal violations total query reference mass respectively, making easier distinguish expected mass destruction solver non-convergence.

title: somalign 0.99.2
text: vignette("algorithm") gains new Limitations OT correction section covering barycentric contraction (correction target conditional mean contracts toward dense reference nodes grows epsilon), unpaired OT design (mass distributions, individual samples, matched — anchor-based methods exploit sample-level pairing), uncalibrated novelty score (match_fraction reflects epsilon/rho regime much true novel population).

title: somalign 0.99.1
text: somalign_fit() default epsilon changed 0.05 0.5. cost matrix now normalised median positive entry Sinkhorn kernel computed, making epsilon scale- dimension-invariant across feature sets different size spread. Users set epsilon explicitly relative raw codebook distances re-tune normalised scale (inspect diagnostics$solver$cost_scale).

title: somalign 0.99.1
text: somalign_query() gains codebook_space argument (default "reference_scaled"). user-supplied som_query provided, setting codebook_space = "raw" instructs somalign_query() re-scale codebook reference-scaled space, mirroring existing behaviour somalign_reference(). somalign_fit() diagnostics now include diagnostics$solver$converged (logical), diagnostics$solver$final_delta (final iterate change), diagnostics$solver$cost_scale (cost normalisation factor).

title: somalign 0.99.1
text: somalign_fit() now emits warning 50 % query mass destroyed transport, 50 % query samples fall outside reference distance thresholds. non-finite Sinkhorn iterate delta now triggers explicit warning instead finishing silently (previously, NaN delta suppressed early-break condition non-convergence warning). somalign_reference_from_nodes() now emits informational messages label probabilities distance quantiles supplied, users aware label transfer outside-reference detection disabled. internal OT input validator now warns query reference node masses zero, preventing silent -zero transport plan. Fixed silent fallback .somalign_thresholds(): requested distance-quantile column absent, warning now emitted rather silently substituting third column position.

title: somalign 0.99.0
text: Added somalign_train_reference() somalign_reference() train wrap existing kohonen SOM fixed reference object, including node mass, per-node label probability, per-node distance quantile summaries. Added somalign_reference_from_nodes() reconstruct reference object pre-computed node-level artifacts (codebook, center, scale, masses, label probabilities, distance quantiles) without needing original data. Added somalign_query() scale query data reference parameters, optionally train query SOM, return somalign_query object containing per-sample node assignments distances. Added somalign_fit() align query SOM fixed reference SOM via codebook-level unbalanced entropic optimal transport. Uses internal pure-R generalized Sinkhorn solver; "auto" retained compatibility alias internal solver. Added somalign_results() extract per-sample data frame direct reference projection columns (canonical) transport-corrected projection columns (auxiliary, annotation visualisation). Added somalign_diagnostics() return solver, OT plan, node-level, projection diagnostics somalign_fit object. Added somalign_sensitivity_grid() sweep grid OT hyperparameters (epsilon, rho_query, rho_ref) return summary data frame tuning stability assessment. Supports optional parallelisation via BiocParallel::bplapply() parallel = TRUE.
